name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: At 2125 S Collins St, Arlington, TX 76010, Loanstar Title Loans can be reached at +1 817-276-4512.
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a vehicle title loan provider. The company positions itself as helping working Americans access emergency cash by leveraging equity in their vehicles. The Fort Worth location at 8304 Camp Bowie is one of five branches serving the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex, with additional locations in Haltom City, Richland Hills, Burleson, and another Fort Worth location on E Lancaster Ave.

The company's core offering is auto title loans, which require only three items: a vehicle, government-issued photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. Loan amounts reach up to $10,000. LoanStar accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. The company provides fee schedules and disclosure documents for multiple loan amounts ($600, $1,200, $2,000) in both single and multiple installment formats, indicating transparent pricing disclosure as required by the Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner (OCCC).

LoanStar differentiates through its multi-location presence in Texas, consistent operating hours across locations (10 AM–6 PM weekdays, 9 AM–2 PM Saturdays), and its long operational history. The company maintains online account access, online payment capabilities, and provides a toll-free phone line (877-511-CASH) for inquiries. They also offer bilingual support (ES option visible on website) and have publicly available fee schedules and OCCC notices.

Title loans are a high-cost emergency lending option carrying significant financial risk. While LoanStar provides required disclosures and operates under state regulation, borrowers should understand that title loans typically carry steep interest rates and fees. Failure to repay can result in vehicle repossession. This product is best suited only for consumers with genuine emergency short-term cash needs who have exhausted lower-cost alternatives and fully understand the repossession risk.
pros: Large loan amounts available up to $10,000, suitable for significant emergency expenses
Quick funding claimed (same-day or next-day available for emergency cash category)
Multiple convenient locations in DFW area (5+ locations listed)
Consistent operating hours across locations including Saturday availability
Accepts multiple payment methods (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Transparent fee schedules publicly available for three loan tiers
Online account access and online payment options for convenience
25+ years of operational history in the industry
cons: Title loans carry very high interest rates and fees, making them expensive debt (specific rates not disclosed on website)
Risk of vehicle repossession if loan is not repaid, eliminating transportation for working borrowers
Loan terms and APR rates not disclosed on the main location page, requiring customers to call or review separate fee schedules
Available only in Texas, limiting geographic accessibility
No information provided about alternative lower-cost options or financial counseling
best_for: Working individuals with immediate emergency cash needs ($1,000–$10,000) who own vehicles with clear titles
Borrowers unable to qualify for personal loans or credit-based lending due to credit limitations
Short-term cash needs where the borrower can repay within weeks to months without risking vehicle repossession
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best for working Texans facing genuine emergency cash shortfalls of $1,000–$10,000 who own vehicles outright. However, the critical caveat is that title loans are a high-cost, high-risk product where failure to repay within weeks results in vehicle repossession, potentially destroying the borrower's transportation and income. This product should only be considered after exhausting credit-based personal loans, credit lines, family loans, and non-profit emergency assistance programs.
services: In-store payments at five DFW locations
Payment methods: cash, check, debit card, Western Union
Phone-based loan inquiries and application support
Fee disclosure documents and OCCC notices
Extended Saturday hours at multiple locations
